items.question_text: Complete: The operational status of ASDE systems must be determined during the _____ briefing, or as soon as possible after assuming responsibility for the associated position.
items.explanation: The operational status of ASDE must be checked during the relief briefing, not after the position is fully assumed.

items.question_text: Under 3-6-1, ASDE systems are used to augment what primary control method?
items.explanation: ASDE augments visual observation of aircraft and vehicle movements on runways, taxiways, and other movement areas. It is an augmentation, not a replacement for visual observation.

choices.text: Visual observation of aircraft and vehicle movements.
Flight plan tracking and amendment processing.

items.question_text: Under 3-6-2, what are the three methods for correlating an ASDE target with positive identification?
items.explanation: The three methods are: pilot/vehicle operator position report, controller's visual observation, or an identified target on ASR or CTRD.

choices.text: ATCBI code, transponder altitude, and ground speed readout.
Pilot/vehicle position report, visual observation, or ASR/CTRD target correlation.
Flight plan cross-check, ATIS code verification, and controller handoff data.
Radar beacon identification, ADS-B correlation, and flight progress strip match.

items.question_text: Under 3-6-3, ASDE information may be used to formulate what type of ATC instructions to aircraft and vehicles on the movement area?
items.explanation: ASDE data may be used to formulate both clearances and control instructions. It's not limited to one type.

items.question_text: When an ASDE system generates a safety logic alert, what must the controller do first?
items.explanation: The controller must immediately assess the situation visually and via the ASDE display, then take appropriate action. The visual assessment comes first before any control instruction.

choices.text: Immediately issue a stop instruction to all aircraft and vehicles on the movement area.
Acknowledge the alert on the system console and wait for it to clear automatically.
Assume the alert is valid and take immediate corrective action without assessment.
Immediately assess the situation visually and as presented on the ASDE display, then take appropriate action.
